title: [v2.2.1-rhel] rmi: don't break when the image is missing a manifest
body:
From 3.1 and later: in `libpod/image/Image.Remove()`, don't go looking for the image's parent before actually deleting the image.  If the attempt to find the image's parent fails for any reason, we're basically stuck, when we could fail in a way that at least gets the caller closer to where they wanted to be.

From 3.1 and later: in `libpod/Runtime.RemoveImage()`, if we can't determine if an image has children because of some error, assume that it doesn't instead of bailing out.

From 3.1 and later: in pkg/domain/infra/abi.ImageEngine.Remove(), when attempting to remove all images, if we encounter an error checking if a given image has children, log a warning, and assume that it doesn't have any instead of failing, which would leave us unable to remove the image without resetting everything.

New for this branch: in `libpod/image/layerTree.toOCI()`, if we can't read the image's configuration in OCI format, log the error and return a `nil` pointer instead of returning the error.

Combined, these changes allow removal of an image that's lost its manifest for whatever reason, as we're seeing in https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1884043.

comments.body: System tests pass on f33 (I ran them on my laptop, because this branch doesn't run any actual tests)
We should let Jindrich know about this one, given it's only targeted for Openshift, not RHEL.
